description_long: This object is a detailed model of a brown crayfish, featuring a segmented body with a robust cephalothorax and a long, flexible abdomen. The exoskeleton is textured and appears hard, with a matte to slightly rough finish, and the coloration ranges from dark brown to lighter, reddish-brown hues. The crayfish has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front, each covered in small bumps and ridges, and four pairs of walking legs extending from the thorax. Two long antennae are visible, projecting forward from the head, and the eyes are positioned on short stalks. The tail fan is well-defined, with individual segments and setae visible. The overall size is small, as indicated by the color calibration cube, suggesting it could fit easily in a human hand.
description: A small, brown crayfish with a segmented body, large claws, and visible antennae, featuring a hard, textured exoskeleton.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crayfish, highlighting the large claws, segmented abdomen, and walking legs.
description_view_1: Rear view focusing on the tail fan and the symmetrical arrangement of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the other profile of the crayfish, with both claws and antennae visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the claws, antennae, and the placement of the eyes on short stalks.
